commit.message:
perf: eliminate O(log T + k) sqrt calls per BVH safety query (#209)

* Initial plan

* Eliminate per-node/per-triangle sqrt calls in BVH traversal

Work in squared-distance space throughout PointToMeshDistance traversal
and take a single std::sqrt() in BVHLowerBoundDistance() after traversal
completes. This avoids O(log T + k) sqrt calls per safety query."

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/79af0930-9e88-49ff-85f4-9774df0adfa5

* Fix doc comments to reflect squared-distance convention

- Update class-level doc: ComputeDistance() returns squared distance;
  callers must take std::sqrt to recover Euclidean distance.
- Update Accept() doc: clarify it stores the squared point-to-triangle
  distance, not the Euclidean distance.

commit.message:
Make AABB availability a class invariant of G4OCCTSolid (#210)

* Initial plan

* refactor: make AABB availability a class invariant of G4OCCTSolid

- Change fCachedBounds from std::optional<AxisAlignedBounds> to plain
  AxisAlignedBounds (value-initialized)
- Throw std::invalid_argument in ComputeBounds() when the bounding box
  is void instead of setting fCachedBounds = std::nullopt
- Remove kFallbackExtentMin / kFallbackExtentMax fallback constants
- Simplify all consumers of fCachedBounds:
  - AABBLowerBound(): drop !has_value() guard, access fields directly
  - DistanceToIn(p): drop std::isfinite() guard
  - Inside(): drop has_value() conditional
  - GetExtent(), BoundingLimits(), CalculateExtent(): drop fallback branches
- Update doc comments in header for fCachedBounds, AABBLowerBound(),
  ComputeBounds(), and SetOCCTShape()
- Convert FallbackWhenAABBUnavailable test to RejectsShapeWithNoGeometry
  using EXPECT_THROW to verify construction-time rejection

for more information, see https://pre-commit.ci

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix heredoc delimiter quoting: apply quote-removal before matching closing line (#320)

The flash lexer stores heredoc delimiter words verbatim (e.g. `<<'EOF'`
→ `HereDoc("'EOF'")`), but bash's closing delimiter is the result of
quote removal on that word — just `EOF`. The dparser was comparing raw
closing-line tokens against the unstripped delimiter, so quoted heredocs
never closed.

## Changes

- **`src/dparser.rs`** — adds `strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes(delim)`
implementing POSIX quote-removal:
  - Single-quoted spans: `'FOO'` → `FOO`
- Double-quoted spans with inner backslash handling per POSIX (`$`, `` `
``, `"`, `\`, newline are special; others keep the backslash)
- Unquoted backslash escapes: `\X` → `X`; lone trailing `\` kept
literally
  - Mixed/partial quoting: `E'O'F` → `EOF`
- Applied at the `HereDoc`/`HereDocDash` match arm before pushing to the
heredocs queue

- **`src/command_acceptance.rs`** — adds
`test_here_documents_quoted_delimiter` covering all four quoting forms
(`'…'`, `"…"`, `\`, mixed) and the `<<-` dash variant

- **`src/dparser.rs` tests** — adds
`test_strip_heredoc_delimiter_quotes` (unit) and per-form annotation
tests verifying `IsOpening`/`IsClosing` pairing

```bash
# Previously: heredoc never closed, will_bash_accept_buffer returned false
cat <<'EOF'
hello
EOF        # ← closing line didn't match "'EOF'" stored in queue
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Heredoc denim quoting</issue_title>
> <issue_description>I don't currently handle heredoc delim quoting.
From the bash reference:
> 
> 
> The format of here-documents is:
> 
> [n]<<[−]word
>         here-document
> delimiter
> The shell does not perform parameter and variable expansion, command
substitution, arithmetic expansion, or filename expansion on word.
> 
> If any part of word is quoted, the delimiter is the result of quote
removal on word, and the lines in the here-document are not expanded. If
word is unquoted, delimiter is word itself, and the here-document text
is treated similarly to a double-quoted string: all lines of the
here-document are subjected to parameter expansion, command
substitution, and arithmetic expansion, the character sequence \newline
is treated literally, and ‘\’ must be used to quote the characters ‘\’,
‘$’, and ‘`’; however, double quote characters have no special meaning.
> 
> 
> Write some test cases in dparser that use a quoted heredoc. Single
quotes, double quotes, and backlashes , and combinations. Add some
simple examples to the command_acceptance module.
> 
> Then see if the contents is appropriately parsed. And try and improve
the parser where required.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

feat(ui): brand child profile setup

Replace BrandSplashLogo hero card with DashboardIllustrationCard pattern
in app/(parent)/child-add.tsx. Removes unused imports and style rules.
No functional changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix GitHub Pages 404s for reports and missing callgrind links in bench report (#207)

* Initial plan

* Fix GitHub Pages 404s and missing callgrind links in bench report

Three issues fixed in ci.yml:

1. Download ci-reports to docs/ (not docs/reports/) in deploy job.
   The artifact is uploaded with two paths (reports/ and bench_raw.json),
   so GitHub Actions preserves the reports/ prefix in the artifact.
   Downloading to docs/reports/ caused double-nesting at
   docs/reports/reports/... (404 for point_cloud_viewer.html and
   test-results.md). Downloading to docs/ maps reports/ correctly to
   docs/reports/.

2. Add family field to each callgrind matrix fixture so the
   family/id key format (e.g. direct-primitives/g4box-box-20x30x40-v1)
   matches what generate_bench_report.py uses when looking up callgrind
   links. Previously only the bare id was used as the key, causing
   callgrind_links.get(fixture_id) to always return None.

3. Update callgrind_links JSON key from bare id to family/id format.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/wdconinc/G4OCCT/sessions/29cfa731-76d4-4fcc-9fde-1e231f4f8941

fix: PRB Generator — full symmetric partial-create recovery

Handles all 4 partial-success scenarios:
1. Both succeed         → full create + link (unchanged)
2. Defect ✓ Story ✗    → show 'Retry Story Only' button pre-filled with defect key
3. Story  ✓ Defect ✗   → show 'Retry Defect Only' button pre-filled with story key
4. Both already exist   → guard: error if both existing keys are filled

'Existing Defect' and 'Existing Story' input fields on the Create form:
- Fill one to skip that issue's creation and go straight to create+link
- Clear both for normal full creation flow
- Button label updates to reflect mode (Defect Only / Story Only / Both)
- prbRetryStoryOnly() mirrors prbRetryDefectOnly() symmetrically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB auto-fix string→option format error + Retry Defect Only button

prbCreateIssue() now handles two auto-fix types in one retry pass:
- 'not on the appropriate screen' → field removed from payload
- 'no String-argument constructor from String value' → field auto-converted
  from plain string to {value: string} (fixes customfield_10253 = 'Production')

prbRetryDefectOnly(storyKey): new function for the partial-success case
where story was created but defect failed. Shows 'Retry Defect Only' button
pre-filled with the existing story key — creates only the defect and links it.

PRB._lastCreate stores {defectPayload, storyPayload, linkType, jiraUrl} so
retry has full context without re-fetching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): build cerebro CLI — enables Phase 8 smoke test

Commands:
  cerebro start                  — poll Linear, auto-spawn agents
  cerebro spawn <proj> <issue>   — spawn agent for specific issue
  cerebro status                 — list active sessions
  cerebro send <session> <msg>   — queue message to running agent

Core:
  - config.ts: loads agent-orchestrator.yaml, walks up to find repo root
  - session-store.ts: persists sessions + assignment-log.jsonl
  - linear-tracker.ts: @linear/sdk wrapper (getIssue, listOpen, comment)
  - orchestrator.ts: model routing, conductor spec/plan, CLAUDE.md + opencode.jsonc
    writer, git worktree creation, agent + terminal spawner, memory/working update

Binary linked globally as 'cerebro'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: PRB Generator — correct Jira field format for option, priority, issuelink

prbFormatFieldValue corrections:
- option:    {name:val} -> {value:val}  (Jira custom selects use 'value', not 'name')
- priority:  numeric values now sent as {id:val} instead of {name:val}
             (stored value '4' is a Jira ID, not a priority name)
- issuelink: {key:val} -> plain string  (Epic Link / Feature Key expects bare key)
- array:     items now {value:} instead of {name:} for multi-select custom fields

prbInferSchemaType() helper:
- If Jira reports schema.type='string' but field has allowedValues, infer 'option'
  (fixes customfield_10253 and similar misdetected fields on future imports)
- Detects epic-link custom fields and maps to 'issuelink'

Action required for existing templates:
- customfield_10253 stored as 'string' type needs to be changed to 'Option'
  in Field Builder > Settings (one-time fix for existing saved templates)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: PRB auto-strip screen-incompatible fields and retry

When Jira rejects fields with 'not on the appropriate screen', automatically
strip those fields and retry the create — fully transparent to the user.

On success, shows which fields were auto-removed (e.g. customfield_10265,
customfield_10254 on Defect; customfield_10253 on Story).
On retry failure, debugBlock shows the stripped payload actually sent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: switch Gemini model to 3.1 preview

refactor: eliminate duplicate billing-window logic and rename boolean variable

- Extract private computeEndOfMonthStandardDominanceStats helper so that
  both detectsEndOfMonthStandardDominance and computeBillingCycleInsight
  derive results from a single calculation, preventing future drift.

commit.message:
fix: PRB Generator — robust error display with full Jira response

- Rewrote prbCreate() promise chain: defect and story errors captured independently
  (previously, defect failure would shadow story error or swallow it entirely)
- Added 'Show Jira response' debug collapse alongside payload debug in error display
- New captureError() preserves e.responseData from relay error objects
- Added console.log for all payloads sent and responses received
- console.error with full response data on any Jira API rejection
- Error display now shows both the formatted error AND the raw Jira response body
  so users can see exactly WHY their payload was rejected

commit.message:
fix: compute avgRetention as weighted period-level KPI

Change computeAverageRetention to accept the raw data array and compute
totalReturning / totalActive across all days, giving a true period-level
retention rate. The previous unweighted mean of already-rounded daily
percentages could diverge significantly from this KPI on days with very
different traffic volumes. Update callers in CLIAdoptionTrendChart and
AdoptionTrendChart; remove the now-unused retentionRates variable from
AdoptionTrendChart.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract detectsEndOfMonthStandardDominance as pure predicate

Decouple scanQuotaExhaustion (flag scanner) from computeBillingCycleInsight
(UI chart insight) by extracting the core billing-cycle detection logic into
a shared pure boolean predicate. Both callers now reference the predicate
directly, so future changes to chart copy or insight shape won't silently
alter flagging behaviour.

commit.message:
B2: Apply quick-win balance optimizations across 5 config files (#47)

Conservative manual balance pass over externalized JSON configs:

- Woodcutting patterns: Fix difficulty-to-reward monotonicity (e.g.,
  Perfect Straight 3.0x→15.0x, Four of a Kind 10.0x→3.5x, reduce
  Eight of a Kind from 55.0x→30.0x, increase straights/sets)
- Crafting durability: Reduce grant 500→300 (10:1→6:1 ratio)
- Scouting: Rebalance asymmetry from (-15%,+30%) to (-20%,+25%)
- Mining: Narrow stamina cost from (50,350) to (80,200)
- Rest: Reduce overpowered recovery (health_cost_stamina 900-1200→500-700,
  stamina_cost_health 600-800→400-550)
- Combat: Reduce health_start_damage from (1000,1300) to (700,950)
- Milestone fishing: Widen valid_range from (-50,+50) to (-80,+100)

Test updates: 4 durability grant assertions updated (500→300).
All 146 tests pass. Coverage 82.37%→83.16%.

Co-authored-by: RobbingDaHood <robbingdahood@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix/nightly builds (#60)

* chore: bump container image gnome-49 → gnome-50

GNOME 50 is the current stable release. Using the gnome-50 Flathub
infra image pre-caches the GNOME 50 SDK, improving build performance
for apps targeting org.gnome.Platform//50 (e.g. Saturn).

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via GitHub Copilot
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nightly): push to main directly + switch thunderbird to FTP URL

- Replace PR workflow with direct push to main. GITHUB_TOKEN cannot
  trigger other workflows via events, so we explicitly dispatch build.yml
  via 'gh workflow run' after pushing (requires actions:write permission).
  This eliminates the PR backlog that was blocking daily builds.

- Switch Thunderbird download from download-installer.cdn.mozilla.net to
  ftp.mozilla.org. The CDN caused HTTP/2 stream errors (curl exit code 92)
  on 2026-03-18, leaving the sha256 stale. FTP is the canonical stable source
  (same server Firefox nightly already uses).

- Add --retry 3 --retry-delay 5 to all curl downloads for resilience.

- Update thunderbird-nightly manifest sha256 to current value (force-refresh
  after CDN failure left it ~2 weeks stale).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(thunderbird-nightly): correct x86_64 sha256

Stale sha256 from previous session caused flatpak-builder to reject
the download. Correct value fetched from Mozilla's own checksums file:
ftp.mozilla.org/.../thunderbird-150.0a1.en-US.linux-x86_64.checksums

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): curl -f to fail on HTTP errors; drop e2e-install from comment

- curl -sL silently writes HTML error page on 404/503, producing a
  wrong sha256. Switch to curl -fsSL with explicit failure check.
- build.yml header comment referenced a non-existent e2e-install job.

Addresses Copilot review comments on PR #60.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: testhub
repository.description: Experimental OCI Flatpak remote — prototype for Flathub OCI transition

commit.message:
fix: JSONB double-encoding in credential save

The save() method was calling json.dumps(credentials) before passing
to asyncpg, which has a registered JSONB codec that calls json.dumps()
again internally. This stored a JSON string instead of a JSON object,
causing credentials_json->>'email' to return NULL.

This broke the entire Gmail webhook pipeline because find_by_email()
uses the ->> operator and could never find Gmail credentials.

Fix: pass the dict directly and let asyncpg's JSONB codec handle
encoding. Also fixed 9 existing double-encoded rows in production DB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor storage content-type handling of ServeDirectURL (#36804)

* replace raw url.Values by *storage.ServeDirectOptions
* implement content-type in azblob
* implement content-disposition in azblob
* add tests for content types in response
* http.MethodPut for azure now allows implementing servedirect uploads

repository.description: Git with a cup of tea! Painless self-hosted all-in-one software development service, including Git hosting, code review, team collaboration, package registry and CI/CD

commit.message:
feat(test): add maestro parent smoke flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make CLI build workflow always trigger on PRs with path-change detection (#464)

Remove path filters from pull_request trigger so the workflow runs on every
PR to main. Add a check-changes job using dorny/paths-filter to detect
CLI-relevant file changes. The build-and-validate job is conditional on
that output, so it skips cleanly when no relevant files changed — making
this workflow suitable as a PR required status check.

commit.message:
fix: resolve all actionlint issues across workflow files (#459)

- cli-build.yml: remove merge conflict markers, use matrix strategy, keep diagnostics step
- check-models.yml: quote $GITHUB_OUTPUT, use grep -c instead of grep|wc -l
- cli-publish.yml: group redirects with { } >> file (SC2129)
- copilot-setup-steps.yml: convert ARGS string to bash array (SC2086)
- release.yml: quote $GITHUB_OUTPUT/$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, group redirects, use find instead of ls
- sync-release-notes.yml: quote $GITHUB_OUTPUT
- sync-toolnames.yml: quote ${GITHUB_WORKSPACE} and $GITHUB_OUTPUT
- validate-skills.yml: quote $GITHUB_OUTPUT

commit.message:
Fix items-to-goal: use blow XP per item, not glass needed

Add itemsToBlowForGoal() which calculates XP remaining / XP per blow
item instead of incorrectly using glassNeeded() (molten glass to make).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show items to goal in glassblowing overlay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add fuzzy case/accent-insensitive name matching for OCR member identification

Replace all 6 exact-substring name comparisons with _names_match() which:
- Normalizes to lowercase and strips accents (é→e, ô→o, etc.)
- Checks substring containment first
- Falls back to SequenceMatcher fuzzy ratio (≥80% threshold)

This correctly matches 'MARYLINE LAMARCHE' to 'Marylin Lamarche' (97% similar)
and 'HÉLOÏSE CÔTÉ' to 'Heloise Cote'.

Applies to both scan session review and single-receipt review paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Pre-fill sub-budgets from previous year on budget year creation

When creating a new budget year, the inline sub-budget formset is now
pre-populated with the previous year's active sub-budgets (names, amounts,
trace codes, sort order). The treasurer can modify, delete, or add
sub-budgets before saving. If no previous year exists, seed defaults are
shown instead.

Removed the seed_default_categories checkbox (replaced by visible formset).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Convert bullet lists to prose in chapter-13

Convert all standalone bullet-point lists outside blockquotes to
integrated prose paragraphs with natural transition phrases, per
editorial guidelines. All clinical facts preserved; numbered lists,
tables, and blockquote callouts left unchanged.
